summary: Do not ship: the RED proof is sound and P-30 is fixed, but the normative `Entry` interface cannot compile as written. Simpler alternative: apply one shared absence-classification helper directly at existing call sites with a pass-local tally, avoiding the child-module wrapper surface. Open question: none.

findings.title: The planned Entry filesystem methods are inaccessible to reconcile.rs
findings.body: `Entry` lives in the new child module `reconcile::entry`. The plan explicitly marks `snapshot`, `name`, and `class` as `pub(super)`, but leaves all six filesystem methods private. Later normative code in the parent `reconcile.rs` calls these methods, which Rust rejects with E0624. An engineer following this interface cannot reach the acceptance stage without improvising a visibility change absent from the plan.
findings.recommendation: Declare `file_type`, `metadata`, `read`, `remove`, `rename_into`, and `rename_durable_to` as `pub(super)` while keeping `seen` private. Also show the lifetime-correct `impl<'v> Entry<'v>` interface and reflect these method visibilities in the import/scope map.

next_steps: Re-run the plan review; the committed RED record does not need regeneration.
